coverage: 100% of reachable node types across the 43-group watertight Bolt closure.
validation: The authored 13-pass heal branch is manifold in both Blender and GN-VM. Blender's native OpenVDB result varied from 16,526-16,598 vertices across ten sequential runs; the stored 16,570 / 16,572 reference is one valid schedule result. The deterministic browser result is 16,222 / 16,224 (2.10% below that reference) with 100% node coverage. Raycast now follows Blender's float32 normalization, watertight triangle test, BLI_kdopbvh construction, and direction-dependent traversal. Re-importing the current pass-one browser mesh into Blender and sampling the same 43 x 43 x 71 SDF lattice matches Blender's inside/outside classification at 131,278 of 131,279 samples (99.9992%); eight of the previous nine coincident-surface BVH ties are resolved. The sole remaining sign difference is coupled to one compiler-sensitive n-gon tessellation tie, while the larger controlled pass-two topology gap comes from the browser surface-net representation rather than the scalar field. The full 13-pass validation takes about 153 seconds on the validation machine.
